channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US issues Level 4 travel advisory for 23 countries, including Nigeria regions
item.description: The US Department of State has updated its global travel advisory, placing 23 countries under its highest warning level (Level 4: Do Not Travel) due to severe security risks and limited consular assis...

item.title: Premier Doug Ford Inspects Thunder Bay Amid Wildfire Evacuations
item.description: Premier Doug Ford visited Thunder Bay, Ontario, to assess the situation as wildfires in northern Ontario continue to force evacuations. The Canadian Press report highlights that many evacuees have sou...

item.title: LAPD Detective Alleges Retaliation After Investigating Suspicious Death
item.description: An LAPD detective has filed a lawsuit against the Los Angeles Police Department, alleging he faced retaliation for investigating the death of Amelia Salehpour. The case centers on conflicting accounts...

item.title: Trump administration weakens endangered species protections with policy changes
item.description: The Trump administration has implemented two new policies that weaken the Endangered Species Act (ESA), raising concerns among environmental advocates. The first policy rescinds the 'blanket rule' fro...

item.title: Government declares holidays for local anniversary celebrations
item.description: The Philippine government has announced special non-working days in five regions to commemorate local anniversaries and cultural festivals. Proclamation 1349 designates July 20 as a holiday in Labanga...

item.title: Waymo Resumes San Francisco Robotaxi Service After Temporary Pause Due to Power Outage
item.description: Waymo, the Alphabet-owned autonomous vehicle company, temporarily paused its robotaxi service in San Francisco for approximately one hour due to a power outage affecting around 7,000 PG&E customers. T...

item.title: Argentina and England Clash in Nations Championship Rugby Match
item.description: The article details the intense rugby match between Argentina and England in the Nations Championship, highlighting the historical rivalry between the two nations. It mentions the previous football ri...

item.title: Seven U.S. Aid Workers in Quarantine After Ebola Exposure in Congo
item.description: Seven American aid workers have been placed in quarantine at a U.S.-run facility in Kenya following potential exposure to the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo, according to Samaritan’s ...

item.title: Aitch's Charity Climb and Documentary Highlight Down's Syndrome Awareness
item.description: Aitch, the Manchester-based rapper, has unveiled a Netflix documentary titled 'Aitch: Don’t Be Afraid' documenting his Mount Kilimanjaro charity climb. The film, directed by Aaron Fitzmaurice and Harr...

item.title: McIlroy criticizes DeChambeau's conduct at British Open
item.description: Rory McIlroy has publicly criticized Bryson DeChambeau's actions during the British Open, accusing him of attempting to disrupt the tournament's proceedings. The incident occurred after DeChambeau was...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli Claims Pole Position at Belgian Grand Prix
item.description: Kimi Antonelli, the 19-year-old Italian driver for Mercedes, secured pole position for the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix after a thrilling qualifying session. In a dramatic finish, Antonelli edged ou...

item.title: Nigeria's Archery Team Competes in African Championships with Record Participation
item.description: Nigeria has sent its largest-ever archery team to the 15th African Archery Championships in Oran, Algeria, with 18 athletes competing across six divisions. The Nigeria Archery Federation (NAFED) descr...

item.title: US military casualties in Jordan as Iran escalates regional conflict
item.description: Two US service members have been killed and another is missing following an Iranian attack on a Jordanian military base, marking the first direct Iranian casualties of US troops since the war began. T...

item.title: Lok Sabha Speaker approves merger of Shiv Sena rebels with Shinde Sena and TMC rebels with NCPI
item.description: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) rebels with Eknath Shinde's Sena faction, while allocating separate seating for 20 Trinamool Congress (TMC) rebels joining the...

item.title: DNA showcases electrifying debut album performances
item.description: The article highlights DNA's debut album showcase, where the group delivered high-energy performances that captivated audiences. The event, held on July 19, 2026, featured a mix of musical styles and ...

item.title: Community efforts expand as Calgary searches for missing 11-year-old boy
item.description: Calgary police have expanded their search for an 11-year-old boy named Parker, who has been missing since Thursday. The search now involves the entire city, with residents urged to check their propert...

item.title: Federal government rejects $42m funding for Northern Rivers rail trail
item.description: The federal government has rejected a $42m grant request to complete the 54km missing link of the Northern Rivers rail trail, despite strong support from local councils. The project, which would conne...

item.title: 72nd National Film Awards: 'Article 370' and Savarkar Biopic Win Top Honors
item.description: The 72nd National Film Awards celebrated several films, with 'Article 370' and the Savarkar biopic receiving top honors. Aditya Jambhale's film 'Article 370' was named best feature, while Yami Gautam ...

item.title: Tanzania promotes unity and youth empowerment on Mandela Day
item.description: Tanzania is observing Mandela Day by emphasizing the importance of unity, volunteerism, and youth empowerment as part of national development efforts. The event, held on July 18, 2026, highlights the ...

item.title: Public speculation surrounds Tom Brady and Logan Paul's on-stage altercation at Fanatics Fest
item.description: Tom Brady and Logan Paul's on-stage altercation at Fanatics Fest has sparked widespread speculation about its authenticity. During the event in New York on July 17, Brady slapped Paul in the face afte...
